summary: Tested Islamic inheritance calculator updates (فرض، رد، تعصيب). Backend 6/6 passing (3 direct calculator tests + 3 API endpoint tests). All required new fields (base_share_fraction, base_share_arabic, radd_fraction, final_share_fraction, share_type) are present in API responses. Frontend code inspection confirms 9 columns are rendered in InheritanceCalculatorDialog.js. Frontend UI navigation deep test partial (login worked, but reaching the calculator dialog requires department selection + existing pending aids).
action_items: Backend logic is correct and validated. No fixes needed.

Main agent may want to add data-testid attributes on the calculator-open button and on header <th> cells in InheritanceCalculatorDialog.js so future UI automation can deterministically reach the dialog without navigating through department selection.

Recommend full manual / e2e verification of the printed case form (caseform_html.py) - server-side string rendering of beneficiaries table looks correct (uses base_share_arabic + رد, final_share_fraction, share_type) but no automated test executes the HTML generation path.
critical_code_review_comments: inheritance_calculator.py: math is correct — verified for (1) wife+4 daughters (wife 1/8 فرض, daughters 2/3 + رد splitting the remainder by their farḍ proportion), (2) husband+mother+daughter (husband 1/4 فرض no radd, mother 1/6 + رد, daughter 1/2 + رد), and (3) wife+son+2 daughters (wife 1/8 فرض, son+daughters تعصيب with 2:1:1 ratio). Total distributed equals input within < 0.5 EGP rounding.
Server.py AidBeneficiary model (lines 608-622) correctly adds the 5 new fields with sensible defaults; backward compatibility fields (percentage, percentage_arabic, inheritance_type) preserved.
InheritanceCalculatorDialog.js (lines 262-318) renders 9 columns as required, with proper fallback to legacy field names (result.base_share_fraction || result.base_share, etc.) — defensive coding.
caseform_html.py (lines 144-176) builds the printed table with the new fields and a 'X + رد' display format. Confirms integration with calculator output.
seed_data_creation: None. Tests used existing aid from default department.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend logic + API endpoints are fully validated. If retesting frontend UI: login then navigate via /departments → click TAKAFUL card → then go to aids/pending. Calculator button is opened from each pending aid row. The 9-column table is present in code and confirmed via code review.
rca of the issue: N/A — feature works as designed. Tested all three Islamic inheritance scenarios from the request and field naming matches Pydantic model and frontend rendering.
